# Spec context: §4.20 — Order of abc constructs

## Source

- Upstream: <https://abcnotation.com/wiki/abc:standard:v2.1#order_of_abc_constructs>
- Cached at: `crates/kaijutsu-abc/docs/abc-spec-cache.md` (line 1548)
- Section: 4. The tune body
- Subsection: 4.20 Order of abc constructs

Reproduced verbatim from the ABC v2.1 standard by Chris Walshaw,
under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 — see `tests/fixtures/spec/README.md`.

## Preceding prose

> The order of abc constructs for a note is: **, **, */* (e.g. Irish roll, staccato marker or up/downbow), **, **, **, **, i.e. `~^c'3` or even `"Gm7"v.=G,2`.
> Each [tie symbol](#ties_and_slurs "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵"), `-`, should come immediately after a note group but may be followed by a space, i.e. `=G,2-` . Open and close chord delimiters, `[` and `]`, should enclose entire note sequences (except for chord symbols), e.g.
